source_title: The Claude Code Leak: A Complete Technical & Security Investigation
why_not_counted_as_peer_reviewed_support: Bounded Crossref and OpenAlex metadata show this source analyzes a March 2026 Claude Code source-map / source-code leak and separately mentions CVE-2026-21852, not the approval-bypass and network-exfiltration path tracked as CVE-2025-55284. It sharpens same-platform literature coverage, but it is a different incident line and cannot close the row-level peer-reviewed gap for LIMEN-000003.
paper_thesis_use: Useful for reviewer-safe disambiguation prose showing that same-platform scholarship exists but must still be split by incident line before it can support the live row.

source_title: What 2025 taught us about AI agent security: A practitioner's guide to the incidents shaping enterprise adoption
why_not_counted_as_peer_reviewed_support: Bounded Crossref abstract and OpenAlex metadata show a published journal article that includes Claude Code among major platforms and summarizes recurring patterns such as prompt injection, trust-boundary weakness, and data exfiltration, but the metadata checked this cycle do not expose an exact CVE-2025-55284 mapping or row-level technical analysis. Publication status alone is therefore not enough to count as exact peer-reviewed support for the live row.
paper_thesis_use: Useful for a methods/limitations paragraph showing that published security synthesis has reached Claude Code at platform level while exact row-level scholarly closure remains open.
